metadata.name: F1 Chatbot Knowledge Base
metadata.note: This is broad F1 knowledge, not literally every F1 fact. Update season-specific facts regularly.

identity.description: Formula 1 is the highest class of international single-seater circuit racing, governed by the FIA.
identity.basic_goal: Drivers and teams compete across Grands Prix to score points and win the Drivers' and Constructors' Championships.

quick_answers.what is f1: Formula 1 is the top level of single-seater motor racing. Drivers race highly advanced cars around circuits and street tracks around the world.
quick_answers.what is a grand prix: A Grand Prix is a Formula 1 race weekend. It usually includes practice, qualifying, and the race.
quick_answers.what is qualifying: Qualifying decides the starting order for the race. The fastest driver usually starts on pole position.
quick_answers.what is pole position: Pole position is first place on the starting grid, earned by setting the fastest qualifying lap.
quick_answers.what is drs: DRS means Drag Reduction System. It opens a flap on the rear wing to reduce drag and help a driver overtake on certain straights.
quick_answers.what is a pit stop: A pit stop is when a driver enters the pit lane so the team can change tyres or fix damage.
quick_answers.what is a safety car: A safety car slows the race when there is danger on track, such as a crash or debris.
quick_answers.what is a virtual safety car: A Virtual Safety Car slows all drivers to a controlled speed without putting a physical safety car on track.
quick_answers.what are slick tyres: Slick tyres are dry-weather tyres with no tread. They give maximum grip on a dry track.
quick_answers.what are intermediates: Intermediate tyres are used for damp or lightly wet conditions.
quick_answers.what are full wets: Full wet tyres are used in heavy rain and standing water.
quick_answers.what is dirty air: Dirty air is turbulent air from the car ahead. It reduces downforce and makes the following car harder to drive.
quick_answers.what is slipstream: Slipstream is reduced air resistance behind another car, helping the following car gain speed on straights.
quick_answers.what is undercut: An undercut is when a driver pits earlier than a rival to use fresher tyres and gain time.
quick_answers.what is overcut: An overcut is when a driver stays out longer than a rival, hoping to gain time before pitting.
quick_answers.what is parc ferme: Parc fermé is the period after qualifying when teams are restricted from changing major car settings.
quick_answers.what is a sprint: A Sprint is a shorter race held on selected weekends. It gives points and adds extra competitive running before the Grand Prix.
quick_answers.what is a constructor: A constructor is the team that designs and races the car, such as Ferrari, Mercedes, McLaren, or Red Bull.
quick_answers.what is the constructors championship: The Constructors' Championship is the title for the team with the most points across both drivers.
quick_answers.what is the drivers championship: The Drivers' Championship is the title for the driver with the most points across the season.

drivers_championship.description: The Drivers' Championship is awarded to the driver who scores the most points during the season.

constructors_championship.description: The Constructors' Championship is awarded to the team whose two drivers score the most combined points.

points_system.note: From 2025, the extra point for fastest lap was removed.

standard_weekend: Practice sessions help teams learn tyre behaviour and setup.
The Grand Prix is the main race where full points are awarded.
sprint_weekend: Sprint weekends include a shorter Sprint race.
Sprint points are awarded to the top finishers.
The Sprint does not replace the main Grand Prix.

flags.green: Track is clear; racing conditions apply.
flags.yellow: Danger ahead; drivers must slow down and overtaking is restricted.
flags.double_yellow: Serious danger; drivers must slow significantly and be ready to stop.
flags.red: Session stopped due to danger, crash, weather, or track issue.
flags.blue: A slower car is being lapped and must let faster cars pass.
flags.black: Driver is disqualified or must return to the pits immediately.
flags.black_and_white: Warning for unsportsmanlike driving or track limits.

dry_compounds.soft: Fastest dry tyre, usually wears out quickest.
dry_compounds.medium: Balanced dry tyre with good speed and durability.
dry_compounds.hard: Most durable dry tyre, usually slower to warm up.

wet_compounds.intermediate: For damp or lightly wet tracks.
wet_compounds.full_wet: For heavy rain and standing water.

tyres.strategy: Teams choose tyre strategies based on wear, temperature, track position, safety cars, and weather.

car_parts.front_wing: Creates front downforce and helps control airflow.
car_parts.rear_wing: Creates rear downforce and includes the DRS flap.
car_parts.floor: A major source of downforce through ground effect.
car_parts.diffuser: Helps accelerate air under the car and create downforce.
car_parts.sidepods: Guide airflow and help cool the car.
car_parts.halo: A safety structure protecting the driver's head.
car_parts.power_unit: Hybrid engine system combining internal combustion and electrical energy recovery.
car_parts.brakes: Carbon braking system designed for extreme temperatures and high stopping power.
car_parts.steering_wheel: Controls gears, radio, engine modes, brake balance, energy deployment, and more.

racing_terms.apex: The inside point of a corner where the driver aims the car.
racing_terms.brake_balance: How braking force is split between front and rear wheels.
racing_terms.degradation: Loss of tyre performance over time.
racing_terms.graining: When rubber tears and sticks to the tyre surface, reducing grip.
racing_terms.blistering: When tyres overheat and the rubber surface is damaged.
racing_terms.lock_up: When a wheel stops rotating under braking, causing a flat spot.
racing_terms.flat_spot: A damaged area on a tyre caused by locking up.
racing_terms.track_limits: Rules defining the legal racing surface. Drivers can be penalized for repeatedly exceeding them.
racing_terms.stewards: Officials who investigate incidents and issue penalties.
racing_terms.drive_through_penalty: A penalty requiring the driver to drive through the pit lane without stopping.
racing_terms.time_penalty: Extra time added to a driver's race result or served at a pit stop.
racing_terms.fastest_lap: The quickest lap set during the race.
racing_terms.formation_lap: The lap before the race start where drivers warm tyres and brakes.
racing_terms.out_lap: The lap immediately after leaving the pits.
racing_terms.in_lap: The lap before entering the pits.
racing_terms.push_lap: A lap where the driver drives at maximum pace.
racing_terms.cool_down_lap: A slower lap to cool tyres, brakes, and engine.

strategy.undercut: Pit earlier than a rival to use fresh tyres and gain time.
strategy.overcut: Stay out longer than a rival to gain track position or use clear air.
strategy.one_stop: A strategy with one pit stop.
strategy.two_stop: A strategy with two pit stops, usually faster if tyre degradation is high.
strategy.track_position: Where a car is physically placed on track; important because overtaking can be difficult.
strategy.clean_air: Air with little turbulence, giving the car better downforce and tyre life.
strategy.dirty_air: Turbulent air behind another car, making the following car harder to drive.
strategy.safety_car_strategy: Teams may pit during a safety car because less time is lost at reduced race speeds.

max verstappen.known_for: Aggressive speed, race craft, consistency, and multiple world championships.
max verstappen.summary: Max Verstappen is a Dutch Formula 1 driver and one of the most successful drivers of the modern era.

lewis hamilton.summary: Lewis Hamilton is a seven-time Formula 1 World Champion, known for his speed, consistency, wet-weather skill, and activism.

charles leclerc.summary: Charles Leclerc is known for his qualifying speed, Ferrari career, and strong one-lap pace.

lando norris.summary: Lando Norris is a McLaren driver known for speed, race craft, and strong performances in the modern era.

oscar piastri.summary: Oscar Piastri is a McLaren driver known for calm race execution, technical feedback, and rapid development.

george russell.summary: George Russell is a Mercedes driver known for qualifying speed, consistency, and race intelligence.

kimi antonelli.summary: Kimi Antonelli is a highly rated Italian driver associated with Mercedes in the current F1 era.

fernando alonso.summary: Fernando Alonso is a two-time World Champion known for race craft, longevity, and adaptability.

sebastian vettel.summary: Sebastian Vettel is a four-time World Champion best known for his Red Bull dominance and later Ferrari career.

michael schumacher.summary: Michael Schumacher is a seven-time World Champion and one of the most influential drivers in F1 history.

ayrton senna.summary: Ayrton Senna was a three-time World Champion famous for qualifying brilliance, wet-weather skill, and intensity.

niki lauda.summary: Niki Lauda was a three-time World Champion known for bravery, intelligence, and his comeback after a serious crash in 1976.

alain prost.summary: Alain Prost was a four-time World Champion known as 'The Professor' for his strategic driving style.

juan manuel fangio.summary: Juan Manuel Fangio was a five-time World Champion and a dominant figure in early F1.

ferrari.summary: Ferrari is the oldest and most famous team in Formula 1, known for its red cars and passionate fanbase.

mclaren.summary: McLaren is a historic British team with many drivers' and constructors' titles.

mercedes.summary: Mercedes dominated much of the turbo-hybrid era from 2014 onward.

red bull.summary: Red Bull Racing became a modern powerhouse, winning titles with Sebastian Vettel and Max Verstappen.

aston martin.full_name: Aston Martin Aramco Formula One Team
aston martin.summary: Aston Martin is a British-branded F1 team aiming to become a front-running constructor.

alpine.summary: Alpine is Renault's works Formula 1 team.

haas.summary: Haas is an American-owned Formula 1 team.

williams.summary: Williams is a historic British team with many championships, now rebuilding competitiveness.

sauber.full_name: Stake F1 Team Kick Sauber / Sauber
sauber.summary: Sauber is a long-running Swiss-based team, associated with Audi's future F1 project.

racing bulls.full_name: Visa Cash App Racing Bulls / Racing Bulls
racing bulls.summary: Racing Bulls is Red Bull's sister team, historically known under names including Toro Rosso and AlphaTauri.

famous_races_and_events.monaco grand prix: One of the most prestigious races, held on narrow streets where qualifying and track position are extremely important.
famous_races_and_events.british grand prix: A historic race often held at Silverstone, one of F1's classic circuits.
famous_races_and_events.italian grand prix: Usually held at Monza, known as the Temple of Speed.
famous_races_and_events.spa francorchamps: A classic circuit in Belgium famous for Eau Rouge/Raidillon and unpredictable weather.
famous_races_and_events.singapore grand prix: A demanding night street race with heat, humidity, and walls close to the cars.
famous_races_and_events.abu dhabi grand prix: Often the season finale, held at Yas Marina Circuit.
famous_races_and_events.las vegas grand prix: A modern street race featuring long straights and night racing.

circuits.monza: High-speed Italian circuit with long straights and heavy braking zones.
circuits.monaco: Tight street circuit where overtaking is very difficult.
circuits.silverstone: Fast British circuit with iconic corners like Maggotts, Becketts, and Copse.
circuits.spa: Long Belgian circuit with elevation change and variable weather.
circuits.suzuka: Technical Japanese circuit with figure-eight layout and famous esses.
circuits.interlagos: Brazilian circuit known for elevation change and dramatic races.
circuits.bahrain: Desert circuit often used early in the season, with heavy braking and tyre degradation.
circuits.jeddah: Fast street circuit in Saudi Arabia.
circuits.austin cota: Circuit of the Americas, a modern track in Texas with elevation change and varied corners.
circuits.yas marina: Abu Dhabi circuit often used as a season finale.

technical_concepts.downforce: Aerodynamic force pushing the car into the track, increasing grip.
technical_concepts.drag: Air resistance that slows the car down.
technical_concepts.ground_effect: Downforce generated by airflow under the car.
technical_concepts.porpoising: A bouncing effect caused by unstable ground-effect aerodynamics.
technical_concepts.ers: Energy Recovery System, which recovers energy and deploys it for extra power.
technical_concepts.mguk: Motor Generator Unit-Kinetic, recovers energy from braking.
technical_concepts.mguh: Motor Generator Unit-Heat, recovers energy from exhaust heat; used in older hybrid power unit regulations.
technical_concepts.brake_by_wire: Electronic rear braking system that balances mechanical and energy recovery braking.
technical_concepts.drag_reduction_system: DRS opens a flap in the rear wing to reduce drag and increase straight-line speed.

penalties.five_second_penalty: A common penalty added to race time or served at a pit stop.
penalties.ten_second_penalty: A larger time penalty for more serious incidents.
penalties.drive_through: Driver must pass through pit lane without stopping.
penalties.stop_go: Driver must stop in pit lane for a set time before continuing.
penalties.grid_penalty: Driver starts lower on the grid, often due to component changes or driving offences.
penalties.disqualification: Driver is removed from the classification, usually for serious rule breaches.

notable_eras.1950s: Early championship years dominated by drivers like Fangio.
notable_eras.1960s: Rise of British teams and engineering innovation.
notable_eras.1970s: Safety concerns, powerful personalities, and technical experimentation.
notable_eras.1980s: Turbo era, Senna-Prost rivalry, and increasing professionalism.
notable_eras.1990s: Schumacher's rise and major safety improvements after 1994.
notable_eras.2000s: Ferrari-Schumacher dominance, then Renault, McLaren, and Brawn success.
notable_eras.2010s: Red Bull-Vettel dominance followed by Mercedes hybrid-era dominance.
notable_eras.2020s: Verstappen and Red Bull dominance followed by renewed competition from McLaren, Ferrari, and Mercedes.

beginner_explanations.why teams pit: Teams pit mainly to change tyres. Fresh tyres can be much faster, but a pit stop costs time, so strategy is about choosing the best moment.
beginner_explanations.why qualifying matters: Starting higher makes the race easier because overtaking can be difficult, especially on narrow tracks.
beginner_explanations.why f1 cars are so fast: F1 cars are fast because they are light, powerful, aerodynamic, and generate huge downforce.
beginner_explanations.why teammates race each other: Both drivers score for the same team, but they also compete individually in the Drivers' Championship.
beginner_explanations.why strategy matters: Even the fastest car can lose if tyre choices, pit timing, or safety car decisions are wrong.

chatbot_behavior_rules.focus: Stay focused on Formula 1 unless the user asks for a comparison.
chatbot_behavior_rules.uncertainty: If the knowledge base does not contain the answer, say that the knowledge base may need updating.
chatbot_behavior_rules.length: Keep normal answers to 2-5 sentences unless the user asks for detail.
chatbot_behavior_rules.examples: Use simple examples when explaining rules, strategy, or technical topics.
